resources.string: Let\'s set up your knowledge companion in a few easy steps
Select the language model provider you want to configure
Configure your API Key and optional base URL for the selected provider
Choose and set up the language model you want to use
Configure advanced options for the model and API connection
How to get an API Key?
Your API Key will be stored securely on your device and will not be shared with any third parties
Your API Key will be stored in plaintext on your device. This may pose security risks, but we will not share it with any third parties
Indicates the model\'s ability to produce speculative or hypothetical responses, useful for exploratory or creative scenarios that present a range of possible outcomes
Adjusts the model\'s randomness (creativity). Higher values increase output diversity; lower values make responses more focused and deterministic
Indicates whether the model can interact with external tools or services
Specifies how the model selects or configures calls to external tools
Ability of the model to generate multiple independent reply options for the same prompt
Indicates capabilities related to visual tasks, such as image understanding and scene analysis
Supports image-specific vision tasks (e.g., classification, captioning, and basic image understanding)
Supports video processing tasks, including temporal analysis and frame-level understanding
Supports audio-related tasks such as transcription, audio classification, and basic signal analysis
Handles document-oriented tasks, including parsing, summarization, and structured extraction
Generates vector embeddings from text for similarity search, clustering, or downstream ML tasks
Generates textual completions or continuations based on a given input context
Indicates support for caching prompts or prompt-derived outputs to reduce latency or repeated work
Provides content-moderation functionality to detect and handle unsafe or policy-sensitive outputs
Supports structured output formats and schema-driven responses
Supports JSON Schema-based validation and generation for structured data exchange
Basic level support for JSON schema: lightweight validation and simple structured outputs
Full support for JSON Schema as defined by json-schema.org, enabling comprehensive validation and complex schemas
Indicates support for OpenAI API endpoints used for LLM operations
Supports the OpenAI Chat Completions endpoint for conversational generation
Supports the OpenAI Responses endpoint for general request/response interactions
GPT series and other models from OpenAI, or OpenAI-compatible providers
Failed to initialize the cryptographic key. Please try again
You are using plaintext storage for sensitive data. This may pose security risks. It is recommended to enable encryption
